messages.content:
YOUR ROLE: You are the partner running a pre-mortem on this market model for a
paying client. The client's own team has already read every number in the
ledger, including every sensitivity band. Your reputation rests on surfacing
what THEY would not list: risks that look benign on the surface but could kill
the venture, argued mechanistically, anchored in the model.

HARD REQUIREMENTS — a hypothesis that misses any of these will be rejected by
a validator before any human reads it:
1. targetNodes: real ledger node ids; perturbation ops only on FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes.
2. mechanism: at least 3 steps (trigger → propagation → model impact), EVERY
   step carrying refs — "ledger:<nodeId>", "digest:<group>#c<n>", or
   "profile:<field>". A step you cannot reference is a step you are guessing.
3. falsifier: the single concrete observation that would kill this risk. If you
   cannot name one, it is not a claim.
4. whyMissable: why a competent founder's own risk list omits this. "It's
   non-obvious" is not an argument; name the surface appearance that hides it.
5. likelihood: a probability with a basis ("evidence" | "base-rate" |
   "judgment") and a rationale. No naked numbers.
6. indicators: at least 2 observable early warnings, each with WHERE to watch
   (a named tender portal, an operator's capex call, a standards docket, a
   vendor's price list) and the reading that should trigger re-assessment.
7. mitigations: at least 2. Where the right move is to buy information, use
   type "information" and name the ledger node it de-risks (voiNodeId) — its
   value will be computed, not asserted.

QUOTAS (per lens): at least one hypothesis self-tagged expectedObviousness
"low", and at least one whose perturbation targets NONE of tamBase /
serviceableFactor / obtainableFactor — the tornado already covers those bands;
look past them.

MINE THE RESIDUALS: the highest-yield material is where sources disagree with
the curated value (the digests' "tensions"), where searches came back empty
(the digests' "absences" — an unquantified market is a risk, not a comfort),
where a derivation's crossCheck diverges from its primary method, and where a
precise-looking value carries maturity "needs-source".

HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

BANNED GENRES (emitting one of these wastes your slot):
- "Find 2 customers to talk to and get an LOI" — an action item, not a risk. It names no node, no mechanism, no falsifier.
- "Do research on customer buying behaviour" — a homework assignment any consultant could write for any company.
- "Competition is strong / the market is concentrated" — the ledger already states CR3. Restating a fact is not a risk; the risk is a MECHANISM that concentration enables.
- "The market may be smaller than expected" — the tamBase band (240–520) already says this. Only worth saying if you name WHICH derivation hop fails and WHY.
- "Regulatory changes could impact the business" — name the instrument, the compliance artifact it requires, and the date it lands, or drop it.
- "The band's low end might be true" with no new reason — the band is already in the model; you are paid for what is NOT in the model.

GOLD EXEMPLAR (the target register — do NOT repeat its content):
{
 "id": "busway-hollowing-hyperscale",
 "title": "Busway + OCP power shelves hollow out the hyperscale 44% from inside the scope boundary",
 "narrative": "The model excludes busbars and power shelves as out-of-scope substitutes — a scoping footnote. But hyperscale is 44% of TAM, and it is precisely the segment whose new AI-density builds are moving power distribution from rack PDUs to overhead busway feeding OCP-style in-rack power shelves. If that architecture becomes the default for new CE hyperscale capacity, the largest slice of this TAM is not 'ours to win slowly' — it is structurally shrinking while the headline number stays flat. The exclusion list quietly became the market thesis.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "seg.hyperscale"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "New hyperscale AI-density builds adopt busway + in-rack DC power shelves instead of discrete rack PDUs",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c?",
    "ledger:seg.hyperscale"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "CE hyperscale capacity additions (the growth the 9% CAGR is made of) carry falling rack-PDU content per MW; replacement demand in legacy halls does not offset it",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:shape.cagr",
    "digest:tam-base#c?"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The hyperscale share of the rack-PDU market shrinks in absolute terms — seg.hyperscale 0.44 overstates the addressable slice for a new entrant's window",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:seg.hyperscale"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Substitutes were explicitly considered and excluded — the boundary decision looks handled. The kill path is not 'we misjudged the boundary' but 'the boundary moved into the biggest segment while the TAM base year stayed put'.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "2025–26 CE hyperscale fit-out specs (or Schneider/Vertiv rack-power order mixes) showing intelligent rack PDUs holding share in new AI-density halls.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.35,
  "rationale": "OCP power-shelf adoption is documented for AI racks, but CE colocation and enterprise retrofits (56% of TAM) keep buying rack PDUs; timing within a 12-month window is uncertain.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "seg.hyperscale",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.22,
   "note": "≈half of the hyperscale slice migrates to busway/shelf architectures"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Rack-power architecture specified in new CE hyperscale tenders (busway/shelf vs PDU)",
   "where": "Frankfurt/Amsterdam data-center fit-out tenders; OCP regional summit deployment talks",
   "threshold": "≥2 flagship CE builds specifying shelf-based distribution",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Incumbent rack-PDU revenue mix commentary (Vertiv/Schneider earnings)",
   "where": "Quarterly earnings calls, rack-power product-line commentary",
   "threshold": "PDU line flat while busway line grows double-digit",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission a CE rack-power architecture survey across the 2026 hyperscale build pipeline",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "seg.hyperscale"
  },
  {
   "action": "Qualify the product line for colocation/enterprise retrofit first, where PDU form-factor is sticky",
   "type": "strategic"
  }
 ]
}

content: {"hypotheses":[{"id":"intelligent-subset-inflation","title":"The €300M base counts the non-intelligent majority of a market the venture cannot sell into","narrative":"The venture sells intelligent rack PDUs, but tamBase anchors on Grand View's $2.81B 'rack PDU' figure — and Grand View itself states the NON-intelligent segment held the largest revenue share in 2025. IndexBox confirms the split is real by selling 'Rack PDUs' and 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' as separate EU reports. The scope-down chain carefully audited geography and FX but never audited product type: roughly half the €300M base is basic/metered units the product line does not address. TAM, SAM and YAM all inherit a ~2x product-scope inflation while the ledger label ('rack-PDU market, all segments/buyers') reads as if it were scope-matched.","category":"fact","targetNodes":["tamBase"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"Grand View's own segmentation of the $2.81B anchor reports the non-intelligent rack-PDU segment held the largest 2025 revenue share, while the venture's product is intelligent PDUs only","refs":["digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c2","ledger:tamBase","profile:Product"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"Independent sizings of the intelligent-only subset run $2.1B (GIA 2024) to $3.0B (MRF 2025) against $1.7–3.9B for total rack-PDU — and IndexBox maintains distinct EU reports for 'Rack PDUs' vs 'Intelligent Rack PDUs', confirming the vendor community treats them as different products with different bases","refs":["digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c12","digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c13","digest:tam-base#c14"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"The €300M CE base carries the full-category value; the venture-relevant intelligent-only base is materially smaller, and every downstream funnel stage (SAM €165M, YAM €1.65M) inherits the inflation because serviceableFactor haircuts reach, not product scope","refs":["ledger:tamBase","ledger:serviceableFactor"]}],"whyMissable":"The label 'rack-PDU market' looks perfectly scope-matched to a rack-PDU vendor — the refinement cycle even proved its diligence by removing the PDUs+PSUs ceiling, creating the impression definitional contamination was handled. The remaining contamination sits inside the anchor's own segmentation table, one level below the headline number anyone reads.","expectedObviousness":"medium","falsifier":"A scope-pure European source (IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs headline, or an Omdia segment table) showing intelligent/monitored/switched PDUs at ≥75% of European rack-PDU revenue — i.e., Europe's revenue mix is intelligent-dominated even if global unit mix is not.","likelihood":{"value":0.5,"rationale":"The anchor source directly states non-intelligent held the largest revenue share globally; the offset is that intelligent units carry 3–5x ASPs and European colo/hyperscale buyers skew intelligent, so the revenue-weighted European intelligent share plausibly lands at 45–60% rather than below 40%","basis":"evidence"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"tamBase","op":"scale","value":0.55,"note":"Restrict the base to the intelligent-PDU-relevant revenue share of the rack-PDU category (~55% revenue-weighted in Europe)"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Intelligent vs non-intelligent revenue split in the next Grand View / Mordor rack-PDU report vintage","where":"Grand View Research rack-PDU report segment tables; Mordor Intelligence Intelligent PDU report","threshold":"Non-intelligent still ≥45% of European revenue","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"ASP spread between basic/metered and switched/outlet-metered SKUs on incumbent price lists","where":"Vertiv Geist, Raritan/Legrand, and APC published EU price lists","threshold":"Basic-unit volumes dominating distributor stock at <€500 ASP while intelligent SKUs sit >€1,500","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission the IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs report and difference it against the general EU Rack PDUs report to isolate the venture-relevant base","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"Two $4,000 reports collapse the product-scope ambiguity that the ±40% band silently absorbs"},{"action":"Add a metered-only downmarket SKU to the roadmap so the non-intelligent half of the base is addressable rather than excluded","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Converts a TAM-definition problem into a product-line decision"}]},{"id":"europe-30pct-arithmetic-ghost","title":"The 'Europe ≈ 30%' hop is an inserted number; two sourced regional shares imply Europe is ≤24%","narrative":"The single most load-bearing hop in tamBase — Europe's share of the global rack-PDU market — is stated by no source. Grand View publishes only NA = 38%; MarketIntelo puts Asia-Pacific at 38.2% and calls it the DOMINANT region. If both regional leaders hold ~38%, Europe plus rest-of-world share a ~24% residual, and Europe alone plausibly sits at 20–23%, not 30%. The 30% was inferred from NA's share alone, before the APAC datapoint existed in the corpus. A 7-point regional-share error moves the base ~23% by itself, and unlike the tornado's band it is a directional bias, not symmetric noise.","category":"fact","targetNodes":["tamBase"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"No retrieved source states a Europe share of the global rack-PDU market; the chain inserts ≈30% by analogy to Grand View's NA = 38%","refs":["digest:tam-base#c1","ledger:tamBase"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"MarketIntelo independently reports Asia-Pacific dominated the rack-PDU market with 38.2% revenue share; NA 38% + APAC 38.2% leaves ≤23.8% for Europe, MEA and LatAm combined, capping Europe well below the inserted 30%","refs":["digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c7","digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c1"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"tamBase is linear in the Europe-share hop: 30%→23% cuts the CE base ~23%, compounding with (not covered by) the ±20% band, and dragging SAM and YAM down proportionally","refs":["ledger:tamBase","ledger:serviceableFactor"]}],"whyMissable":"The expression reads like sourced arithmetic — '$2.81B × Europe ≈ 30% [est.; NA = 38% per same source]' — so a reviewer sees a citation next to the hop and assumes the 30% is anchored. The bracketed 'est.' is the only tell that the decisive multiplier is invented, and the corroborating APAC datapoint lives in a different publisher's report the model never joined against.","expectedObviousness":"medium","falsifier":"A published regional revenue table for the rack-PDU category (Grand View regional chapter, IndexBox world-vs-EU headline pair, or Omdia) showing Europe at ≥28% of global revenue.","likelihood":{"value":0.4,"rationale":"Two independent sources sum NA+APAC to ~76%, directly constraining Europe below 30%; the counterweight is that MarketIntelo's category scope may differ from Grand View's, so the two shares may not be strictly additive","basis":"evidence"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"tamBase","op":"scale","value":0.77,"note":"Europe share corrected from 30% to ~23% residual; all other hops held constant"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Europe regional revenue share in any rack-PDU-specific report vintage","where":"Grand View rack-PDU report regional segmentation; IndexBox world benchmark report vs EU report headline ratio","threshold":"Europe stated or derivable at <27%","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"EMEA vs Americas vs APAC rack-power revenue mix in incumbent disclosures","where":"Vertiv 10-K segment reporting; Legrand annual report data-center line commentary","threshold":"EMEA rack-power consistently <25% of global segment revenue","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Purchase the IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report and the world benchmark report together to compute Europe's share directly instead of inferring it","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"This is the exact hop the ledger's own promotionPath targets; the affordance table already prices the information at €30M of TAM swing"},{"action":"Rebuild tamBase bottom-up (CE MW × €-per-MW PDU intensity from vendor fit-out BOMs) so the regional-share hop is bypassed entirely","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"A second independent method converts the untestable 30% into a cross-checkable intensity assumption"}]},{"id":"colo-denominator-geography-misallocation","title":"Colo-only apportionment plants the flag in Germany/NL while 35–40% of PDU demand sits in non-colo load with a different country mix","narrative":"Every geo share divides colo MW by colo MW, but rack-PDU demand tracks TOTAL installed IT load — and the non-colo remainder is not distributed like colocation. Germany's installed load (BMWK >2,700MW) exceeds its colo figure by ~1,000MW, Switzerland is self-operation-heavy (installed-basis share 0.13 vs 0.08), and Poland's better-corroborated installed-load figure yields 0.10 vs 0.07. EUDCA confirms the gap is continental: colo+hyperscale is only about two-thirds of Europe's IT power. A venture that builds its Year-1 named-account plan on the colo-derived map will staff Frankfurt and Amsterdam while a third of the actual PDU spend — enterprise and self-operated halls in CH, PL, AT — never appears on its target list.","category":"model-structure","targetNodes":["geo.DE","geo.NL","geo.CH","geo.PL"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"EUDCA: commercial colo+hyperscale provides just over two-thirds of Europe's IT power — so roughly a third of PDU-consuming load is outside the colo scope every geo.* ratio is built on","refs":["digest:geo-region-denominator#c11","ledger:geo.DE"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"The colo-vs-installed divergence is country-specific, not uniform: DE's installed-load method gives 0.42 vs colo 0.50, CH's self-operated stock pushes it to ~0.13 vs 0.08, and PL's triangulated 660MW installed load implies ~0.10 vs 0.07 — the €300M gets reallocated across countries, not just rescaled","refs":["ledger:geo.DE","ledger:geo.CH","ledger:geo.PL"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"The Year-1 plan sells through large operators (colo-centric by construction), so the misallocated map inflates the reachable share of DE/NL and hides demand in CH/PL enterprise halls; the funnel's geography mix shifts against the venture's channel build","refs":["profile:Who is the primary buyer you'll sell through first?","ledger:geo.NL","digest:geo-region-denominator#c14"]}],"whyMissable":"Each geo node already discloses its colo-vs-installed crossCheck divergence, so the issue looks acknowledged — but it is acknowledged as per-country BAND WIDTH, not as a correlated, same-direction reallocation across all countries at once. Bands on independent nodes cannot express 'the whole dimension is measured in the wrong denominator'.","expectedObviousness":"medium","falsifier":"A single-scope, single-source table of total installed IT load for all CE-7 countries (e.g., EUDCA country profiles) whose per-country shares match the current colo-based shares within ±3 percentage points.","likelihood":{"value":0.45,"rationale":"The scope gap is documented in the ledger's own crossChecks for four of seven countries and corroborated continentally by EUDCA; the uncertainty is whether non-colo PDU spend per MW is comparable to colo (enterprise refresh cycles are slower, partially offsetting the reallocation)","basis":"evidence"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"geo.DE","op":"set","value":0.42,"note":"Installed-load method share; mix shift, deliberately not renormalized"},{"nodeId":"geo.NL","op":"set","value":0.24,"note":"NL colo-heavy market loses relative weight under installed-load scope"},{"nodeId":"geo.CH","op":"set","value":0.13,"note":"Self-operated Swiss stock enters the denominator"},{"nodeId":"geo.PL","op":"set","value":0.1,"note":"Mordor/R&M-corroborated 660MW installed load"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Publication of country-level installed IT power (not colo-only) for CE-7","where":"EUDCA 'State of European Data Centres' country profiles; BMWK/Bitkom German landscape updates","threshold":"Any CE country's installed-load share diverging >5pp from its colo share","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Origin of inbound PDU demand by facility type in early pipeline","where":"Venture's own CRM plus PMR Poland commercial-DC series and CBRE Suisse commercial-vs-self-operated splits","threshold":">25% of qualified CE demand coming from enterprise/self-operated sites the colo map does not list","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Reconcile all seven countries onto one installed-load scope before locking Year-1 territory and account assignments","type":"information","voiNodeId":"geo.DE","note":"The DE node carries the largest band swing (−€24M TAM at the low edge) and is the cheapest to resolve — two public sources already bracket it"},{"action":"Add an enterprise/self-operated sales motion (TÜV-certified retrofit bundle) for CH and PL alongside the large-operator channel","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Hedges the plan against the denominator being wrong without waiting for the data"}]},{"id":"stock-vs-flow-intensity-mismatch","title":"tamBase apportions revenue by standing MW, but PDU revenue follows new-build flow — which is concentrating outside CE-7","narrative":"The CE-7 = 39.8%-of-Europe hop divides standing colocation stock by standing stock. But rack-PDU revenue is fit-out-driven: it is earned when MW are delivered or refreshed, not while they sit installed. CBRE shows the flow concentrating away from CE: 57% of 2025's 937MW of new European supply lands in FLAPD (of which only Frankfurt and Amsterdam are CE-7), 85% of take-up is FLAPD, London alone exceeds 1GW and is Europe's largest market, and double-digit growth in secondary markets is going to Milan and Madrid — not Warsaw or Vienna. If CE-7's share of European capacity ADDITIONS is meaningfully below its 39.8% share of stock, the €300M systematically overstates where 2026 PDU purchase orders will actually be written, even if every input number is individually correct.","category":"model-structure","targetNodes":["tamBase"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"European colo revenue-generating activity is flow-shaped: 937MW of new supply in 2025, 57% of it in FLAPD, and 85% of take-up in FLAPD — a distribution measurably different from the standing-stock table the CE-7 ratio is computed from","refs":["digest:geo-region-denominator#c6","digest:geo-region-denominator#c7","digest:geo-region-denominator#c9"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"London (>1GW, Europe's largest market) and Frankfurt together represent roughly half of European supply, and CBRE's tracked secondary-growth markets are Milan and Madrid — so the growth increment that a 9% CAGR is made of accrues disproportionately to non-CE-7 geographies, while CE-7's standing stock skews toward older, lower-density halls with slower PDU refresh","refs":["digest:geo-region-denominator#c3","digest:geo-region-denominator#c8","digest:geo-region-denominator#c19"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"The 39.8% stock ratio overstates CE-7's share of near-term PDU revenue; tamBase should be read as a stock-weighted ceiling, with the flow-weighted (order-relevant) base materially lower — a bias no band edge in the tornado captures because it is a method error, not parameter noise","refs":["ledger:tamBase","digest:geo-region-denominator#c4"]}],"whyMissable":"Stock apportionment is the standard top-down move and every input is sourced and recent — nothing on the page looks estimated. The rot is in an unstated physical assumption (PDU € per standing MW is uniform across regions and vintages) that no source states because no source measures it; you only see it by asking what event actually generates a PDU invoice.","expectedObviousness":"low","falsifier":"CBRE's 2025–26 delivery pipeline (or Statista's 2026-vintage country deltas) showing CE-7 capturing ≥38% of new European colocation MW delivered — i.e., flow share matching stock share.","likelihood":{"value":0.35,"rationale":"The FLAPD concentration of additions and take-up is well documented, but Frankfurt and Amsterdam are themselves FLAPD members inside CE-7, which claws back part of the flow; the net CE-7 flow deficit vs its stock share is real but plausibly 5–10 points rather than catastrophic","basis":"judgment"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"tamBase","op":"scale","value":0.8,"note":"Re-weight the CE-7 share from standing stock toward capacity-additions flow (~32% vs 39.8%)"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"CE-7 share of new European colocation MW delivered per quarter","where":"CBRE 'Europe Data Centres Figures' quarterly PDFs (new supply by market)","threshold":"CE-7 markets (Frankfurt, Amsterdam, Zurich, Warsaw, Vienna, Prague) <35% of tracked deliveries for two consecutive quarters","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Growth differential between CE-7 and non-CE secondary markets","where":"Statista Europe colocation power by country, 2026 vintage vs 2025; CBRE secondary-markets commentary (Milan, Madrid)","threshold":"Non-CE secondary markets adding MW at ≥2x the CE-7 ex-DE/NL rate","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Rebuild the CE base on a two-component model: refresh revenue on standing stock plus fit-out revenue on the CBRE delivery pipeline, priced per MW from vendor BOMs","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"Separates the stock-vs-flow question the current single ratio conflates"},{"action":"Position Year-1 offering around retrofit/refresh of CE-7's older standing stock (where stock-based sizing IS the right measure) rather than new-build tenders","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Aligns go-to-market with the component of the TAM the model measures correctly"}]},{"id":"band-top-contaminated-comparator","title":"The €360M ceiling was set with the same contaminated-category trick it claimed to remove — the real base may sit above the band and the plan is sized for the wrong market","narrative":"Refinement cycle 1 lowered the band top from €495M to €360M by comparing the BIS ceiling's 21% growth against a '10–12% intelligent-PDU CAGR (IndexBox)'. But that 10–12% figure comes from IndexBox's €8.5–9.5B EU 'Power and Cable Management' report — a category several times broader than rack PDUs — so the correction that purged one contaminated number imported another. Meanwhile Germany alone is forecast at $509.6M in a PDU category by 2031, already exceeding the entire CE-7 base, and the global anchors span $1.7–3.94B, a 2.3x spread wider than the band. If the true CE base sits at €400M+, the failure mode is not embarrassment — it is a Year-1 plan whose certification scope, channel headcount, and manufacturing commitments were sized for €165M of SAM while incumbents scale into the €80–120M the model never showed the board.","category":"fact","targetNodes":["tamBase"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"The band-top-lowering comparator ('10–12% intelligent-PDU CAGR, IndexBox') is sourced from the EU Power and Cable Management report — a €8.5–9.5B multi-category basket — not a rack-PDU report, replicating the exact definitional bundling the refinement flagged in the BIS ceiling","refs":["digest:tam-base#c4","ledger:tamBase"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"Independent evidence points above the band: Germany's broader-PDU forecast alone reaches $509.6M by 2031, and same-category 2025 global anchors span $1.7B to $3.94B — a spread that exceeds the ±20% band and makes the €360M cap an artifact of one contested comparison rather than a bound","refs":["digest:tam-base#c9","digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c4","digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c9"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"With tamBase capped at €360M, SAM and the Year-1 obtainable plan are calibrated low; under-sizing systematically under-provisions certification breadth (serviceable reach) and channel capacity in the launch window, which is unrecoverable in a market where gear is specified 12–24+ months ahead","refs":["ledger:tamBase","ledger:serviceableFactor","ledger:obtainableFactor"]}],"whyMissable":"Downward refinements read as rigor — 'we caught an inflated ceiling and cut it' is exactly what a diligent team wants on the record, so nobody audits the comparator that justified the cut. Upside error also feels safe ('conservative is fine'), hiding that under-sizing misallocates real resources: too-narrow cert scope and too-thin channel for the market that actually exists.","expectedObviousness":"low","falsifier":"The IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report headline (once purchased) landing at or below €360M for the 2025/2026 EU base, confirming the lowered ceiling despite the contaminated comparator.","likelihood":{"value":0.3,"rationale":"Multiple broader-category datapoints point above the band, but each carries the same definitional contamination in the upward direction; the honest read is that the ceiling is unproven rather than proven wrong","basis":"judgment"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"tamBase","op":"set","value":420,"note":"Base above the current band top, consistent with the Germany-alone $509.6M signal and the upper global anchors"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Directly-scoped EU/DE rack-PDU headline figures","where":"IndexBox EU Rack PDUs and Germany Rack PDUs reports ($4,000 each); Omdia rack-PDU vendor share table","threshold":"EU headline >€400M or Germany >€180M","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Incumbent EMEA rack-power growth outrunning the model's 9% CAGR","where":"Vertiv and Legrand quarterly earnings calls, EMEA data-center product-line commentary","threshold":"Two consecutive quarters of >15% YoY EMEA rack-power growth","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission the IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report to fix the base directly — it resolves this hypothesis and the Europe-share hop simultaneously","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"The ledger's own promotionPath; a $4,000 spend against a €60M+ band question"},{"action":"Build the Year-1 operating plan with staged capacity options (certification scope and contract manufacturing volumes expandable at 6-month gates) rather than fixed to the €165M SAM","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Makes the plan robust to the base being above the band without pre-committing capital"}]}]}
